description: A segment of customers who consistently view and purchase across multiple categories, with high engagement metrics and frequent repeat purchases, but have not ordered recently.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
Top purchase categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.1%; Toys: 15.2%
cautions: Segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating approximate behavior-based grouping
No demographic or lifestyle inferences can be made from the data

description: A segment of customers who frequently browse and add items to cart but never complete purchases, with activity concentrated in Books, Beauty, and Toys categories. Last active over a year ago.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 20.2%; Beauty: 19.0%; Toys: 13.8%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores - represents behavior approximation, not distinct natural group
No purchase-related metrics available (all zero or none values)
Last activity over a year ago suggests dormant/inactive status

description: A segment of customers who exhibit high engagement with the site (page views, add-to-cart activity) and nearly universal purchase conversion, but with infrequent purchasing over long intervals and moderate spend. Their browsing and purchasing are moderately concentrated in dominant categories, with partial alignment between viewed and purchased categories.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 23.4%; Beauty: 18.5%; Toys: 17.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 26.8%; Beauty: 23.5%; Toys: 15.2%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores - represents approximate behavioral grouping
No demographic or lifestyle inferences can be made from available data

description: A segment of customers who view many pages, add items to cart at a moderate rate, and consistently purchase across sessions. They exhibit high purchase category focus and perfect view-purchase category alignment, with spending concentrated in Books, Beauty, and Toys.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
Top purchase categories: Books: 26.2%; Beauty: 21.7%; Toys: 15.6%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores; represents behavior-based approximation
No demographic or lifestyle inferences can be made from available data

description: A large segment of customers who frequently browse and purchase across diverse categories, but with no overlap between their most-viewed and most-purchased categories. They exhibit high engagement metrics but have not ordered recently.
evidence: Top view categories: Books (18.6%), Toys (17.2%), Beauty (15.8%)
Top purchase categories: Beauty (24.2%), Books (23.5%), Toys (14.1%)
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores - represents behavioral approximation, not distinct natural group
No demographic or lifestyle inferences can be made from available data
Category match rate of 0.0% indicates complete separation between browsing and purchasing interests

segment_name: Low-Frequency Purchasers with Narrow Purchase Focus
description: A segment of customers who purchase infrequently, with high concentration in a dominant purchase category, despite moderate browsing activity across multiple categories. They have not made a purchase in over two years but consistently add items to cart during sessions.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 19.2%; Beauty: 15.9%; Toys: 15.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 21.8%; Beauty: 20.1%; Toys: 15.9%
cautions: Segment produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating approximate behavior-based grouping rather than distinct natural clusters
No direct correlation between viewed and purchased categories (0.0% match rate)
Last purchase occurred over two years ago (1043.7 days since last order)
